sections.paragraph:
παμμεγέθης, ες (πᾶς, μέγεθος ‘greatness’; Pla., X. et al.; Polyb. 5, 59, 4; Lucian, Charon 20; Herm. Wr. 2, 4a; OGI 619, 6; Ps 67:31 Sym.; TestAbr B 7 p. 111, 10 [Stone p. 70]; 10 p. 114, 22 [Stone p. 76]; GrBar; Philo De Op. Mundi 134 al.; Jos., Ant. 15, 364) immense ἄστερα παμμεγέθη a prodigious star GJs 21:2 (not pap.). Superl. παμμεγεθέστατος (Suda on Γολιάθης) infinitely great, surpassing τὸ παμμεγεθέστατον κράτος (of God.—π. is a divine attribute also in Aberciusins. 14: ἰχθὺς π.) 1 Cl 33:3. Subst. τὸ παμμέγεθες by far the greatest 33:4 (on κατὰ διάνοιαν here s. διάνοια 3).—DELG s.v. μέγας.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Charon Charon , after IV b.c.? b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Aberciusins. Aberciusins. = Aberciusinschrift—List 3

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 754
